name: l7_early_survival_gate
description: At level 7, switch to a survival-first placement policy for the first 12 pieces: avoid creating holes and avoid exposing deep wells before any line-clear setup.
llm_notes: Level 7 has the weakest stability (avg_height=19.56, avg_holes=59.94), and failure episodes end after avg_pieces=1.0 with avg_holes=90.64. A strict early-game gate targets the immediate-collapse regime before scoring policy can matter.

name: tetra_survive_tall_boards_hole_cap
description: When height > 75%, keep conservative mode but add a hard hole cap: reject placements that increase hole count unless they clear lines immediately.
llm_notes: Parent already focuses on tall-board survival. Adding explicit hole-growth control is justified by the failure/success gap (avg_holes 90.64 vs 76.94, delta 13.7) while both operate near terminal height (~19), so hole trajectory is a stronger discriminator than height alone.

name: outlier_entry_pattern_replay
description: Learn and replay opening-to-midgame board patterns from high-surprise outlier episodes to increase the probability of entering the long-survival mode.
llm_notes: Novelty events at level 7 include 243, 206, and 164 lines (surprise up to 5.75), and top 1% outliers average 183.2 lines vs baseline 2.87. This suggests rare but valid trajectories exist; explicitly modeling their entry conditions should improve consistency.
